value: bm project info requires a project NAME argument: 'bm project info main --json'. Without the name, it errors with 'Missing argument NAME'. The --json output includes statistics.isolated_entities (integer), statistics.note_types (dict), statistics.observation_categories (dict), statistics.most_connected_entities (array).

Gardener audit scripts: 4 of 5 gaps solvable with existing MCP tools + better workflow prose. Only regex scope-leak genuinely needs a script. search_notes(entity_types=[observation], query=[[npm:) detects wiki-link violations. Don't over-engineer with 5 scripts when workflow improvements suffice.

raindrop tagging: use javascript + types instead of typescript. The user prefers the ecosystem tag (javascript) for TS-related content, with types as a supplementary tag to indicate the TypeScript/type-safety angle. Keeps discoverability under the main javascript tag.

unsorted-triage skill: collection funnel for triaged bookmarks. Three AI-managed collections: (1) AI-triaged — default landing zone for all triaged unsorted bookmarks (mirrors AI-bookmarked pattern), (2) AI-gold — high-reuse-probability bookmarks promoted during or after triage, (3) AI-archive — low-reuse bookmarks (ephemeral shopping, dead product pages, one-off lookups). User's 13k+ organic library stays untouched. These three collections + AI-bookmarked form a complete AI-managed namespace. Skill must NEVER sort into user-curated collections. Collections created lazily on first skill run, not pre-provisioned.

bm tool read-note has NO --output-format json flag — only returns raw markdown text. The structured observations array is only available via the MCP read_note(output_format='json') tool, not the CLI. Scripts using bm tool cannot get parsed section data.

Dogfood published skills on real data before releasing — static checks catch syntax, only live execution catches semantic leakage (employer name in tag convention example). Build dogfooding into release checklist.

raindrop tagging: ALWAYS load ~/.claude/references/raindrop-tags.md (from /tag-sync) before proposing tags. It has 150 curated tags with counts and characterizations. Avoids redundant find_tags lookups and ensures consistent tag selection. The vocabulary file is the single source of truth for tag naming.

raindrop tag blocklist: ONLY block numeric ratings (5/4/3/2), import artifacts (imported/toread/unread), and Delicious for:* sharing tags. Swedish tags (tankar/butik/nördigt/förening/sverige) and cool/web2.0 are ACTIVE personal curation tags — never block them. The user's 20-year library has deliberate taxonomy that looks like legacy from outside.

unsorted-triage skill: detect 'research bursts' (temporal clusters of 3+ bookmarks within ~30min) across ALL collections, not just unsorted. Each burst implies a research narrative. Tag bursts with research-burst + topic tags. Cross-reference burst topics against Basic Memory to suggest /package-intel or concept note creation for undocumented topics. Burst detection is the key heuristic — it recovers intent from otherwise unlabeled bookmarks. For sorted collections, bursts reveal recent research interests even when bookmarks are already organized.

AskUserQuestion IS a real Claude Code tool but MUST NOT be listed in skill allowed-tools. Known bug (anthropics/claude-code#30363, #30523): listing it causes silent auto-approve via alwaysAllowRules, returning empty answers without showing UI. Safe to reference by name in workflow prose, but our convention is generic phrasing ('Wait for user response before proceeding') for consistency with sibling skills.

unsorted-triage skill: URL normalization for dedup. Strip known tracking params before URL comparison to auto-detect duplicates. Four patterns cover ~90%: (1) Google Shopping ?referer=google-shopping&gclid=..., (2) Facebook ?fbclid=...&utm_*, (3) affiliate ?srsltid=..., (4) anchor fragments #section and path variants (// vs /). Libraries like clean-url or a regex strip of utm_*, fbclid, gclid, srsltid params. Run dedup detection as first step before any tagging work.

2026-04-14 session: built full Gustafson web component collection (14 npm notes + reference:web-component-starter + person note with 54 relations). Created people-intel skill (v0.26.1, committed e269897). Created Commons Trap + Absorption Response Taxonomy + Cross-root ARIA standard notes. Key insight: platform absorbs functionality not opinions; Light DOM is gap-resistant; state management is the permanent framework territory.

bm tool search-notes returns JSON by default (with results array containing title, permalink, content, matched_chunk, metadata). bm tool read-note returns raw markdown only. This asymmetry means search can be scripted with jq but read-note output needs text parsing.

CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT is string substitution by Claude Code, NOT a shell env var. Works in: hooks.json command fields, skill SKILL.md content, .mcp.json, .lsp.json, plugin.json. Does NOT work in agent .md files — agents see the literal string. For script paths in agents, use CWD or hardcode relative to project root.

Raindrop tag/bookmark modifications require explicit user approval via AskUserQuestion before EVERY batch — never auto-execute. The 13k+ library is organically curated over ~20 years; present exact tool calls and affected counts for review.

unsorted-triage skill: support a 'context axis' tag like 040-work that marks WHY something was bookmarked (work vs personal interest), separate from WHAT it's about. User's workplace is 040.se. The tag 040-work distinguishes client-project research from personal learning. This axis is especially useful for research bursts on topics outside the user's established interests.
